claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority; Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with the first question:

**How many children are you responsible for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my child. I have one child - Alex, who is 4 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that context! So you have one child, Alex, who is 4 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, this is helpful for the initial eligibility check. Now I need to understand your living situation better.

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that. Since you live with Alex in the UK, that's an important factor for eligibility.

Now I need to check a few more details about your circumstances:

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or another right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that. As a British citizen, you have the right to reside in the UK, which is important for Child Benefit eligibility.

I have one final question to complete the assessment:

**Is there anyone else claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or has anyone else already been approved to claim Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, yes - Alex's other parent lives with us and they already claim Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that important information. I now have all the details I need to make a determination about your eligibility for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Outcome: Not Eligible**

Unfortunately, you are **not eligible** to claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

**Why:** While you meet most of the eligibility requirements (you live in the UK, you're responsible for Alex who is under 16 and lives with you, and you're a British citizen), there is one key rule that prevents you from claiming: **only one person can get Child Benefit for each child**.

Since Alex's other parent is already receiving Child Benefit for Alex, you cannot also claim for the same child.

**What you can do:**
If you'd like to change who receives the Child Benefit for Alex, you and the other parent could agree to switch, and then contact the Child Benefit Office to arrange this change. Alternatively, if your circumstances change significantly (for example, if Alex were to live solely with you), you could make a new claim at that point.

reasonings: Alex is 4 years old and lives with you in the UK. Normally, this would make you eligible for Child Benefit as you are responsible for a child under 16 living in the UK, and you are a British citizen with the right to reside. However, according to the Child Benefit rules, only one person can get Child Benefit for a child. Since Alex's other parent already claims Child Benefit for Alex, you are not eligible to claim for the same child. If circumstances change or you wish to have someone else claim instead, you would need to contact the Child Benefit Office to arrange a change in who receives the payments.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You do not qualify for Child Benefit for Alex because only one person can receive Child Benefit for each child, and Alex's other parent is already claiming. You would need to contact the Child Benefit Office if you want to change who receives the Child Benefit for Alex.">
